top_findings.summary: Skill instructs users to download and execute a remote script via curl-to-bash without verification, creating supply chain attack risk

spans.why: Direct instruction: 'curl -sSL https://canifi.com/skills/healthgrades/install.sh | bash' - classic supply chain attack pattern

top_findings.summary: Skill claims capabilities (search doctors, read reviews, check credentials) but no implementation files are present to support these claims

spans.why: Network endpoints defined but no implementation shown for advertised capabilities

top_findings.summary: Examples show credential storage in environment variables without security context, potentially normalizing unsafe practices

spans.why: Example: 'canifi-env set HEALTHGRADES_EMAIL "your-email@example.com"' and password storage

missed.why: Critical to examine the downloaded script for actual behavior - needed to assess maliciousness of download-execute pattern
Need to see actual implementation files for advertised capabilities (search, reviews, credentials)
